plan.regime: strong (SPY above MA50/MA200, no high-vol breakdown, VIX 18)
actions_planned: Hold KLAC through earnings (4.37% cushion provides buffer)
No new entries today — discipline over activity, full super-tier list not visible

reflection:
Strong regime day with VIX subsiding (-3.69%). Discovered three of five positions had earnings within 7 days — TER tomorrow, APH and KLAC on the 29th. Closed TER (+2.37%) and APH (+0.7%) where cushions were thinner than typical earnings move size; held KLAC where the +4.37% cushion provides meaningful buffer. Booked small gains on both rather than gamble on prints.

Key lesson encoded as a rule: at entry I should verify earnings is >2 weeks out, not merely >7 days. The 7-day rule technically permits entry at day 8, but that gives the trade no runway to build a cushion before binary risk hits. Today's forced exits weren't bad luck — they were the consequence of entering too close to earnings dates.

Did NOT add new positions despite freeing two slots. The super-tier scan output was partially truncated, and forcing entries with incomplete visibility violates the "no FOMO / build slowly" principle. Strong regime ≠ obligation to be fully invested. Will revisit tomorrow with cleaner data and post-earnings picture on TER.

Evidence-backed rules respected: did not chase any far_from_high or low_volume names. Account preserved capital, locked in two small wins, held three quality names.
